title: SAT Biology - No Repeats
description: 20 SAT-style biology questions with unique content across major biological domains.

questions.questionText: What part of the brain regulates vital functions like heart rate and breathing?
questions.explanation: The medulla controls involuntary activities like heart rate and breathing.

questions.questionText: Which organelle packages and modifies proteins for transport within the cell?
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus processes and packages proteins.

questions.questionText: Which molecule acts as the primary energy currency in cells?
questions.explanation: ATP (adenosine triphosphate) stores and transfers energy for cellular processes.

questions.questionText: What is the function of guard cells in plant leaves?
questions.explanation: Guard cells control the opening and closing of stomata to regulate gas exchange.

questions.questionText: Which biome is characterized by low rainfall, permafrost, and short vegetation?
questions.explanation: Tundras have permafrost, low biodiversity, and cold, dry conditions.

questions.questionText: Which structure allows bacteria to transfer DNA through conjugation?
questions.explanation: Pili are used by bacteria to exchange DNA via conjugation.

questions.questionText: Which base pairs with guanine in DNA?
questions.explanation: In DNA, cytosine pairs with guanine.

questions.questionText: Which evolutionary process results in the formation of new species due to geographic isolation?
questions.explanation: Allopatric speciation occurs when physical barriers separate populations.

questions.questionText: Which part of the eye adjusts to control the amount of light entering?
questions.explanation: The iris contracts or expands to control pupil size and regulate light entry.

questions.questionText: What is the role of ribosomes in the cell?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes build proteins from amino acids.

questions.questionText: Which biomolecule contains nitrogen, carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen and forms enzymes?
questions.explanation: Proteins are made of amino acids and function as enzymes.

questions.questionText: Which plant hormone is responsible for cell elongation and phototropism?
questions.explanation: Auxin promotes elongation and directional growth toward light.

questions.questionText: Which factor is abiotic in an ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Abiotic factors are non-living, such as light, temperature, and water.

questions.questionText: Which structure helps bacteria resist antibiotics?
questions.explanation: Endospores are resistant to heat, chemicals, and antibiotics.

questions.questionText: Which RNA type brings amino acids to the ribosome during translation?
questions.explanation: tRNA carries amino acids during protein synthesis.

questions.questionText: Which structure in Darwin’s finches is an example of adaptive radiation?
questions.explanation: Differences in beak shapes reflect adaptation to diverse food sources.

questions.questionText: What is the main function of the large intestine?
questions.explanation: The large intestine absorbs water and forms feces.

questions.questionText: Which phase of meiosis results in the separation of homologous chromosomes?
questions.explanation: During anaphase I, homologous chromosomes are pulled apart.

questions.questionText: What is the main role of helicase in DNA replication?
questions.explanation: Helicase unwinds the DNA double helix at the replication fork.

questions.questionText: Which plant structure is responsible for absorbing water and minerals?
questions.explanation: Root hairs increase surface area for water and mineral absorption.

rewards.description: Earn 1000 XP for conquering this SAT Biology set!
